items.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
items.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

items.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
items.summary: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

items.title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship in blow to Trump
items.summary: The ruling is a major setback for Donald Trump's immigration agenda, and has been welcomed by civil rights groups.

items.title: Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
items.summary: Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.

items.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
items.summary: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

items.title: Several killed and injured in fire at Antwerp apartment building
items.summary: The fire broke out in the 10-storey block in the Linkeroever district of the Belgian city.

items.title: Roof collapse kills 14 children at Pakistan tuition centre
items.summary: Two people are taken into custody after the incident in the Kahna suburb of Lahore.

items.title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
items.summary: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.

items.title: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
items.summary: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.

items.title: Anthropic says US lifts export ban on its advanced AI tools
items.summary: Fable and Mythos were abruptly suspended in June over concerns that they could be used by hackers.

items.title: US envoys in Doha to meet mediators but not Iranians, Qatar says
items.summary: Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman says no high-level meetings or direct talks between the US and Iran are scheduled.

items.title: Firefighters struggle to contain deadly Greek wildfire
items.summary: More than 100 firefighters are fighting a fire near Thessaloniki which has claimed at least two lives.

items.title: 'Absolute madness': Row over plan to demolish Nazi bunker under Berlin
items.summary: City officials want to build flats on the city centre site but others say it should be preserved as part of Germany's history.

items.title: Thousands of anti-migrant protesters march in South Africa amid heavy police presence
items.summary: Foreigners have already left South Africa ahead of the deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

items.title: US Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes in female school and college sports
items.summary: President Trump called the ruling a "big win", while an LGBT group said it was "heartbreaking".

items.title: Australia sues Amazon for making allegedly unfair contracts with subscribers
items.summary: The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) says Amazon has broken consumer protection law

items.title: Almost 60,000 far-right extremists in Germany, intelligence agency says
items.summary: More than a quarter of those identified are believed to be violent, Germany's domestic intelligence agency says.

items.title: US Supreme Court has dealt heavy defeats to Trump, while expanding his power
items.summary: The birthright ruling brings to an end a Supreme Court term that has delivered the president some key victories.

items.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
items.summary: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

items.title: Why $20 durians are now being sold at half price - or given away for free
items.summary: As Malaysian durians are being sold at throwaway prices, farmers worry about their livelihoods.

items.title: What are US and Japanese soldiers doing in the middle of the Australian bush?
items.summary: The BBC's Katy Watson explains why troops are training in remote Australia, thousands of kilometres from any major conflicts.

items.title: What to know about the looming  deadline on North American free trade
items.summary: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

items.title: Americans react to Supreme Court upholding birthright citizenship
items.summary: The BBC asked Americans how they felt after the Supreme Court's ruling on citizenship for babies born in the US.

items.title: Canadians share hopes and wishes for America's 250th birthday
items.summary: We asked Canadians across the country what they would say and give to their southern neighbour.

items.title: Why are South Korean fans so angry at their football coach?
items.summary: Hong Myung-bo has resigned after South Korea failed to advance to the World Cup knockout stages.

items.title: Watch: Young bald eagle takes first flight in California
items.summary: A popular stream set up by the Friends of Big Bear Valley caught the moment a juvenile eagle nicknamed Luna spread its wings.

items.title: Rescuers keep up search for survivors at collapsed La Guaira building
items.summary: At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.
